framework.name: Agent Payments Protocol (AP2)
framework.description: Open protocol for agent-initiated payments using cryptographically-signed mandates (Intent Mandates and Cart Mandates) to establish verifiable proof of user authorization. Distinguishes between human-present (real-time) and human-absent (delegated) purchase scenarios with different authorization chains. Explicitly designed as payment-method agnostic (cards, stablecoins, real-time bank transfers).
framework.raw_quote: AP2 builds trust by using Mandates—tamper-proof, cryptographically-signed digital contracts that serve as verifiable proof of a user's instructions. These mandates are signed by verifiable credentials (VCs) and act as the foundational evidence for every transaction.

source.title: Announcing Agent Payments Protocol (AP2) | Google Cloud Blog
